<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace test_design_eas_database {

struct Student {
    std::string SNo;
    std::string SName;
    int Sex = 1;
    std::optional<std::string> Birthday;
    std::optional<int> Major;
    float Scorses = 0.0;
    std::optional<std::string> Remark;

    sqlgen::PrimaryKey<"SNo"> pk;
};

struct Course {
    int CID;
    std::string CName;
    std::optional<int> Term = 1;
    std::optional<int> CTime;
    float Credit = 0.0;

    sqlgen::PrimaryKey<"CID", true> pk;
};

struct Score {
    std::string SNo;
    int CID;
    std::optional<float> Score;

    sqlgen::PrimaryKey<"SNo", "CID"> pk;
    sqlgen::ForeignKey<"SNo", "Student"> fk1;
    sqlgen::ForeignKey<"CID", "Course"> fk2;
};

void design_eas_database() {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    sqlgen::create_table<Student>(conn);
    sqlgen::create_table<Course>(conn);
    sqlgen::create_table<Score>(conn);
}

}  // namespace test_design_eas_database
```</answer>

   bool soft_delete_records(const std::vector<int>& ids) {
     // Connect to the database
     auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
     if (!conn_result) {
         return false; // Connection failed

     // Start a transaction
     auto start_transaction_result = conn->execute("BEGIN TRANSACTION;");
     if (!start_transaction_result) {
         return false;
     }

     using namespace sqlgen::literals;
     auto query = sqlgen::update<Document>
                  | sqlgen::set("is_deleted"_c = 1, "deleted_at"_c = sqlgen::transpilation::now())
                  | sqlgen::where("doc_id"_c.in(ids.begin(), ids.end()));

     // Execute the query
     auto result = conn->execute(conn->to_sql(query));
     if (!result) {
         // If there's an error, rollback the transaction
         auto rollback_result = conn->execute("ROLLBACK;");
         if (!rollback_result) {
             // Even rollback failed, we still return false
         }
         return false;
     }

     // Commit the transaction
     auto commit_result = conn->execute("COMMIT;");
     if (!commit_result) {

#pragma once

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int stock;
};

struct Sale {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  double amount;
};

struct SalesSummary {
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string product_name;
  int total_quantity;
  double total_revenue;
};

/**
 * Function: Create a view SalesSummary that aggregates sales by product.
 * 
 * @return true if view creation succeeds, false otherwise
 */
bool create_view_sales_summary() {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;

    auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    if (!conn_result) {
        return false;
    }
    auto conn = conn_result.value();

    auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Sale, Product>(
        where("product_id"_c == "product_id"_c))
        .group_by("name"_c)
        .select(
            "name"_c.as("product_name"),
            sum("quantity"_c).as("total_quantity"),
            sum("amount"_c).as("total_revenue")
        );

    auto create_stmt = sqlgen::create_as<SalesSummary>(query).or_replace();

    return create_stmt(conn);
}

}  // namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary
```

namespace analyze_temporal_data {

  std::optional<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">> end_time;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
};

struct TemporalAnalysis {
  int event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  int start_day;
  int start_month;
  int start_year;
  int start_hour;
  int start_minute;
  int start_second;
  int start_weekday;
  int64_t start_epoch;
  std::optional<double> duration_days;
};

std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data (const std::vector<Event>& events)  {

  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // Create a temporary table
  conn->execute("CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE IF NOT EXISTS TempEvent AS "
                "SELECT event_id, event_name, start_time, end_time, created_at FROM Event LIMIT 0;");

  // Convert events to TempEvent
  std::vector<TempEvent> temp_events;
  for (const auto& e : events) {
    TempEvent te;
    te.event_id = e.event_id.value(); // Assuming PrimaryKey<int> has a value() method
    te.event_name = e.event_name;
    te.start_time = e.start_time;
    te.end_time = e.end_time;
    te.created_at = e.created_at;

  }

                  ta.start_day = row.start_time.day();
                  ta.start_month = row.start_time.month();
                  ta.start_year = row.start_time.year();
                  ta.start_hour = row.start_time.hour();
                  ta.start_minute = row.start_time.minute();
                  ta.start_second = row.start_time.second();
                  ta.start_weekday = row.start_time.weekday();

                  } else {
                    ta.duration_days = std::nullopt;

}

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace analyze_temporal_data {

struct Event {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
  std::optional<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">> end_time;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
};

struct TemporalAnalysis {
  int event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  int start_day;
  int start_month;
  int start_year;
  int start_hour;
  int start_minute;
  int start_second;
  int start_weekday;
  int64_t start_epoch;
  std::optional<double> duration_days;
};

std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data (const std::vector<Event>& events)  {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Create temporary table structure matching Event
  conn->execute("CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE IF NOT EXISTS TempEvent AS "
                "SELECT * FROM Event WHERE 1=0;");
  
  // Insert events into temporary table
  std::vector<std::optional<std::string>> data;
  for (const auto& event : events) {
    data.push_back(std::to_string(event.event_id.value()));
    data.push_back(event.event_name);
    data.push_back(event.start_time.str());
    if (event.end_time) {
      data.push_back(event.end_time->str());
    } else {
      data.push_back(std::nullopt);
    }
    data.push_back(event.created_at.str());
  }
  
  conn->insert("TempEvent", data.begin(), data.end());
  
  // Query the temporary table
  auto result = (sqlgen::read<std::vector<TemporalAnalysis>> 
               | sqlgen::select_from<"TempEvent">(
                    "event_id", "event_name", "start_time", "end_time", "created_at")
               | sqlgen::where("end_time IS NOT NULL")
               | sqlgen::transform([](auto&& row) {
                 TemporalAnalysis analysis;
                 analysis.event_id = std::stoi(row[0]);
                 analysis.event_name = row[1];
                 auto start = sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">::from_str(row[2]);
                 analysis.start_day = start.day();
                 analysis.start_month = start.month();
                 analysis.start_year = start.year();
                 analysis.start_hour = start.hour();
                 analysis.start_minute = start.minute();
                 analysis.start_second = start.second();
                 analysis.start_weekday = start.weekday();
                 analysis.start_epoch = start.unixepoch();
                 if (row[3]) {
                   auto end = sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">::from_str(*row[3]);
                   analysis.duration_days = start.days_between(end);
                 }
                 return analysis;
               }));
  
  return result(conn);
}

}  // namespace analyze_temporal_data

#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace upsert_inventory_items {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
};

/**
 * description: upsert_inventory_items.
 * This function demonstrates how to use upsert (insert or replace) statements in sqlgen write API.
 * It inserts or replaces the given inventory items in the Inventory table.
 */
bool upsert_inventory_items(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  try {
    // Connect to the SQLite database
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    
    // Perform upsert operation
    auto upsert_op = sqlgen::write::upsert(items);
    auto upsert_result = upsert_op(conn);
    if (!upsert_result) {
      return false;
    }

    // Read back all items to verify
    auto read_op = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Inventory>>;
    auto read_result = read_op(conn);
    if (!read_result) {
      return false;
    }
    auto read_items = *read_result;

    // Verify each upserted item matches the input
    std::map<int, Inventory> input_map;
    for (const auto& item : items) {
      input_map[item.item_id] = item;
    }

    for (const auto& input_item : items) {
      auto it = input_map.find(input_item.item_id);
      if (it == input_map.end()) {
        return false;
      }
      const auto& read_item = it->second;
      if (read_item.name != input_item.name || read_item.quantity != input_item.quantity) {
        return false;
      }
    }

    return true;
  } catch (...) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace upsert_inventory_items

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace analyze_social_network {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct Friendship {
  int user1_id;
  int user2_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
};

struct FriendConnection {
  std::string user1_name;
  std::string user2_name;
};

std::vector<FriendConnection> analyze_social_network_impl(const std::vector<User>& users, const std::vector<Friendship>& friendships) {
    // Connect to the database
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

    // Create tables
    conn->execute(sqlgen::create_table<User>.if_not_exists());
    conn->execute(sqlgen::create_table<Friendship>.if_not_exists());

    // Insert data
    conn->execute(sqlgen::insert(users));
    conn->execute(sqlgen::insert(friendships));

    // Build and execute the mutual friends query
    auto mutual_friends_query = sqlgen::select_from<User>("u1")
        .join<Friendship>("f1", "u1.user_id"_c == "f1.user_id1"_c)
        .join<Friendship>("f2", "f1.user_id2"_c == "f2.user_id1"_c)
        .join<User>("u2", "f2.user_id2"_c == "u2.user_id"_c)
        .where("u1.user_id"_c < "u2.user_id"_c)
        .where(sqlgen::exists(
            sqlgen::select_from<Friendship>("f3")
                .where("f3.user_id1"_c == "u1.user_id"_c && "f3.user_id2"_c == "u2.user_id"_c)
        ))
        .group_by("u1.user_id"_c, "u2.user_id"_c)
        .having(sqlgen::count("f1.user_id2"_c) > 0)
        .fields("u1.username"_c, "u2.username"_c, sqlgen::count("f1.user_id2"_c).as("mutual_friends"));

    // Execute the query and return the result
    return conn->template read<std::vector<FriendConnection>>(mutual_friends_query);
}

}  // namespace analyze_social_network

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

/**
 * description: replace_text_in_notes.
 * This function demonstrates how to replace text in notes using sqlgen update API.
 * It replaces all occurrences of "foo" with "bar" in the content column of the Note table.
 * Returns: std::vector<UpdatedNote>.
 */
std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  // Connect to the SQLite database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Perform the update operation using sqlgen's update API
  auto update_result = (sqlgen::update<Note> 
                         | sqlgen::set("content", sqlgen::replace("content", "foo", "bar")))(conn);
  
  // Read the updated notes into a vector of UpdatedNote
  auto notes = sqlgen::read<std::vector<UpdatedNote>>(conn);
  
  // Return the vector of updated notes
  return notes;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
  std::string location;
};

bool inventory_bulk_write_and_read(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn) {
    return false;
  }
  
  // Write the batch of items
  auto write_result = sqlgen::write(conn.value(), items);
  if (!write_result) {
    return false;
  }
  
  // Build the query: read with filter (quantity > 50) and order by quantity descending
  auto query = sqlgen::read<Inventory> 
             | sqlgen::where("quantity"_c > 50) 
             | sqlgen::order_by("quantity"_c.desc());
  
  // Execute the query and check if any items were found
  auto result = query(conn.value());
  if (result && !result.value().empty()) {
    return true;
  } else {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read

We are given the task to write a function named `aggregation_arithmetic` that uses the sqlgen library to construct a query with arithmetic operations on aggregations. The function should:

1. Define a `Product` struct.
2. Construct a query selecting `AVG(price) + 10.0` from `Product`.
3. Return the generated SQL string.

We are provided with the code skeleton and the necessary dependencies.

Steps to implement:

1. Define the `Product` struct as given in the code skeleton.
2. Use the `sqlgen` library to create a table alias for `Product` (using `sqlgen::as<"product">`).
3. Use the `sqlgen::select_from` function to start building the query.
4. Apply the aggregation `AVG` on the `price` column of the `Product` table.
5. Perform the arithmetic operation by adding 10.0 to the result of the aggregation.
6. Convert the resulting query object to SQL using `sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql`.
7. Return the generated SQL string.

Note: The `avg` function is available in the `sqlgen` namespace, and we can use the literal operator `_c` to refer to the column by name.

Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace aggregation_arithmetic {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  double price;
};

/**
 * This function demonstrates arithmetic on aggregations.
 * Returns: std::string.
 */
inline std::string aggregation_arithmetic() {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;
    
    auto query = sqlgen::select_from(
        sqlgen::as<"product">(Product{})
    ) | sqlgen::agg(avg("price"_c) + 10.0);
    
    return sqlgen::sqlite::to_sql(query);
}

}  // namespace aggregation_arithmetic
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace get_top_products {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string product_name;
  std::string category;
  double price;
};

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Date sale_date;
};

struct TopProduct {
  int product_id;
  std::string product_name;
  std::string category;
  double total_sales;
};

/**
 * Description: This function retrieves the top products based on total sales.
 * Returns: std::vector<TopProduct>.
 */
std::vector<TopProduct> get_top_products(size_t limit_count = 10) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Build the query
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product>(
      "product_id"_c,
      "product_name"_c,
      "category"_c,
      sqlgen::transpilation::as<"total_sales">(sqlgen::transpilation::sum("price"_c * "quantity"_c)))
      | sqlgen::transpilation::join<"s", sqlgen::transpilation::JoinType::inner_join>(
          Sales{},
          "product_id"_c == "product_id"_c<"s">)
      | sqlgen::transpilation::group_by("product_id"_c, "product_name"_c, "category"_c)
      | sqlgen::transpilation::order_by(sqlgen::transpilation::desc("total_sales"_c))
      | sqlgen::transpilation::limit(limit_count);

  // Execute the query
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    throw std::runtime_error(conn_result.error().what());
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();

  auto result = query(conn);
  if (!result) {
    throw std::runtime_error(result.error().what());
  }

  // Convert the result to vector of TopProduct
  std::vector<TopProduct> top_products;
  for (const auto& row : *result) {
    TopProduct tp;
    tp.product_id = std::stoi(*std::get<0>(row));
    tp.product_name = *std::get<1>(row);
    tp.category = *std::get<2>(row);
    tp.total_sales = std::stod(*std::get<3>(row));
    top_products.push_back(tp);
  }

  return top_products;
}

}  // namespace get_top_products
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <unordered_map>

namespace update_inventory_stock {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  int stock;
};

bool update_inventory_stock(const std::unordered_map<int, int>& received) {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  auto conn_res = sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_res) return false;

  auto txn_res = begin_transaction(conn_res.value());
  if (!txn_res) return false;
  auto txn = txn_res.value();
  
  for (const auto& [pid, qty] : received) {
    auto stmt = update<Product> | set("stock"_c, "stock"_c + val(qty)) | where("product_id"_c == val(pid));
    auto res = txn.execute(stmt);
    if (!res) {
      return false;
    }
  }
  
  auto commit_res = commit(txn);
  return commit_res.has_value();
}

}  // namespace update_inventory_stock
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace weekday_sales_analysis {

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> sale_time;
};

struct WeekdayStats {
  int weekday;
  int total_quantity;
};

/**
 * Description: This function analyzes sales data to calculate total quantity sold for each weekday.
 * Returns: std::vector<WeekdayStats>.
 */
std::vector<WeekdayStats> weekday_sales_analysis() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Sales>(
      sqlgen::col<"sale_time">.strftime("%w").as("weekday"),
      sqlgen::sum(sqlgen::col<"quantity">).as("total_quantity")
  )
      .group_by("weekday")
      .order_by("weekday")
      .to<std::vector<WeekdayStats>>;
  auto result = query(conn);
  if (result) {
    return result.value();
  } else {
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace weekday_sales_analysis
```</answer>
